sections.recommendation:
Adopt a Resolution authorizing the City Manager to execute a contract and any
necessary amendments with Kala Art Institute for an amount not to exceed $50,000 to
implement a framed artwork purchase plan for two city administration buildings located
at 1947 Center Street and 2180 Milvia Street. The artworks are to be hung in public
areas of civic buildings and will become part of the City’s Civic Art Collection.
sections.fiscal_impacts:
The $50,000 contract will be funded through the Cultural Trust Fund (Fund Code 150-
21-208-252-0000-000-446-612990) in Fiscal Year 2025. Transportation and installation
services, estimated to be $10,000, will be rendered by Gizmo Art Productions (As
Needed Contract #32500079). Installation and transportation costs are funded from
Fund 150-21-208-252-0000-000-446-612990-.
sections.current_situation:
The City is in the process of purchasing a limited number of two-dimensional artworks to
fill in gaps within the existing collection of artworks on display in the public lobbies and
hallways of 2180 Milvia Street and 1947 Center Street, two of the City’s most prominent
city-owned administration buildings. See locations and artworks selected in Attachment
1, Exhibit A: Project Overview: Framed Artwork Purchase Plan for City Buildings.
For many years, the Civic Arts program managed an annual contract with Berkeley’s
Kala Art Institute to produce a rotating Civic Center Exhibition in the City’s buildings
(2180 Milvia and 1947 Center). In 2018, many parts of the Civic Center buildings
became secured and locked -- no longer accessible to the public. After much
discussion, in 2019 the Civic Arts Commission’s Public Art Subcommittee
recommended discontinuing the exhibition because the public could no longer view it.
The annual rotating exhibitions were replaced with curated purchased artwork from Kala
Art Institute for permanent installation. These artworks are part of the city’s substantial

Civic Art Collection which includes outdoor artworks located throughout the community
as well as indoor artworks located within City buildings that have a high degree of public
access and visibility.
In late 2020 (FY21) funding was granted to make additional permanent artwork
purchases to supplement the existing public art collection for the remaining publicly
accessible spaces in 1947 Center Street and 2180 Milvia Street.
Kala Art Institute, located at 2990 San Pablo Avenue, is serving as the curator in
selecting the new artworks. Kala is a Berkeley community arts non-profit organization,

sections.background:
• For many years, the Civic Arts program managed an annual contract with Berkeley’s Kala Art Institute to
produce a rotating Civic Center Exhibition in the City’s buildings (2180 Milvia and 1947 Center).
• In 2018, many parts of the Civic Center buildings became secured and locked -- no longer accessible to the
public. After much discussion, the Civic Arts Commission’s Public Art Subcommittee recommended
discontinuing the exhibition because the public could no longer view it.
• The annual rotating exhibitions were replaced with curated purchased artwork from Kala Art Institute for
permanent installation. These artworks are part of the city’s substantial Civic Art Collection.
• In late 2020 (FY21) funding was granted to make additional permanent artwork purchases to fill in the gaps
within the existing collection for the remaining publicly accessible spaces in 1947 Center and 2180 Milvia.
• Civic Arts staff has worked with curators at the Kala Art Institute to select and purchase artworks and will
implement the following plan (see pages 3-24). Installation will occur in late 2024 - early 2025.
Page 6 of 28

sections.rationale:
The framed artwork purchase plan for city buildings was developed and selected based
upon extensive input from the public art subcommittee of the Civic Arts Commission,
and with the support of a professional curator.
sections.alternatives:
None.
CONTACT PERSON
Mark Salinas, Office of Economic Development, (510) 981-7538

Page 4 of 28
RESOLUTION NO. ##,###-N.S.
CONTRACT: KALA ART INSTITUTE TO IMPLEMENT FRAMED ARTWORK
PURCHASE PLAN FOR CITY BUILDINGS
WHEREAS, the City of Berkeley’s Arts and Culture Plan calls for the enhancement of
Berkeley’s environment by placing temporary and permanent public art in public spaces
throughout the city; and
WHEREAS, the project goal is to purchase a limited number of framed artworks to fill in
gaps within the existing collection of framed art hung in public areas of Civic Center
buildings (lobbies and entry hallways); and
WHEREAS, historically the Civic Arts program managed an annual contract with
Berkeley’s Kala Art Institute to produce a rotating Civic Center Exhibition in the City’s
buildings (2180 Milvia and 1947 Center); and
WHEREAS, the annual rotating exhibitions were replaced with curated purchased artwork
from Kala Art Institute for permanent installation and these artworks are part of the city’s
substantial Civic Art Collection; and
WHEREAS, funding was granted in FY21 to make additional permanent artwork
purchases to supplement the existing public art collection for the remaining publicly
accessible spaces in 1947 Center Street and 2180 Milvia Street; and
WHEREAS, funding for the framed artwork purchase plan is included in the budget for
Cultural Trust Fund (Fund 150) for Fiscal Year 2025; and
WHEREAS, the Civic Arts Commission approved the purchase plan at its regularly
scheduled commission meeting on October 30, 2024; and
NOW THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED by the Council of the City of Berkeley that the
City Manager is authorized to execute a contract and any amendments thereto with Kala
Art Institute to implement a Framed Artwork Purchase Plan for City Buildings for a
